github.copilot.chat.commitMessageGenerat.text: Generate exactly one Conventional Commit subject in the format: <type>(<optional-scope>): <description>.
Use English only. Output the subject only—no body, footer, quotes, markdown, or code block.
Allowed types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert.
Use present tense. Keep the description concise (8-14 words) and do not end with a period.
Describe only observable code, configuration, or file changes. Do not mention intent, motivation, benefits, or impact.
Focus on the 1-2 most significant changes. Avoid filler words, vague adjectives, and long enumerations.
Include a scope only when it is obvious from the changes.
If CHANGELOG.md or any changelog file is updated, match the wording of its corresponding entry.
If the matching changelog entry references an issue, append the issue reference at the end (for example: (#123)).
Example: feat: add SingleEvent and update event API
Counterexample: feat: add event handling improvements for better management and future extensibility
Prefer strong action verbs such as add, remove, update, rename, fix, refactor, optimize, simplify, and replace.
Do not mention implementation details unless they are the primary change.
Avoid generic words such as improve, enhance, adjust, update code, and miscellaneous unless they accurately describe the actual change.
Prefer singular nouns and concise technical terms over verbose phrases.
